name: Identity & Privileged Access Management

useCases.use: All production Azure workloads requiring privileged operations (subscription admin, Key Vault, database admin, network changes)
useCases.dontUse: Non-privileged read-only developer access to non-production sandboxes with no sensitive data

useCases.use: Service-to-service authentication for all cloud-native workloads (replace service account passwords with Managed Identities)
useCases.dontUse: Isolated research or innovation sandboxes with no connection to production or corporate identity plane

useCases.use: Third-party vendor access requiring time-limited, audited privileged access to McCain systems
useCases.dontUse: Consumer-facing application identity (use Azure AD B2C pattern instead)

components: Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) — corporate identity plane for all human and workload identities
Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management (PIM) — just-in-time privileged role activation with approval workflow
Microsoft Entra ID Governance — access reviews, lifecycle workflows, and entitlement management
Azure Managed Identities — system-assigned and user-assigned identities for all Azure service-to-service auth
Microsoft Entra Verified ID — verifiable credentials for third-party vendor identity verification
Microsoft Defender for Identity — identity threat detection (lateral movement, pass-the-hash, Kerberoasting)
Azure Key Vault — certificate and secret management for workload identities
Microsoft Sentinel — identity-based incident detection and SOAR playbooks

guardrails.rule: MFA is mandatory for all human identities — passwordless (FIDO2) required for all privileged users
No standing Owner or Contributor role assignments in production subscriptions — PIM eligible only
All Azure workloads must use Managed Identity for service-to-service auth — no stored secrets in code or config
Quarterly access reviews must be completed for all privileged and sensitive roles — non-response = revoke
Identity audit logs from Entra ID must be streamed to Sentinel — minimum 2-year retention

revisionHistory.author: Sarah Chen
revisionHistory.summary: Initial IAM pattern covering Entra ID, PIM, and Managed Identities

revisionHistory.author: Sarah Chen
revisionHistory.summary: Added Entra ID Governance access reviews, Verified ID for vendors, and Defender for Identity integration

revisionHistory.author: Sarah Chen
revisionHistory.summary: Elevated to Endorsed status after CISO review. Added FIDO2 passwordless mandate for privileged users.

strategicIntent: Establish a unified Identity & Privileged Access Management (IAM/PAM) framework across McCain's entire Azure estate — ensuring that every human and non-human identity is governed by least-privilege access, just-in-time elevation, and continuous verification aligned with Zero Trust principles.
exceptionProcess: Submit Security Exception Request via ServiceNow with CISO approval. Standing privileged access exceptions require formal risk acceptance, compensating controls (enhanced logging, 30-day review cycle), and automatic expiry after 90 days.
interactionSteps: All human identities authenticate via Microsoft Entra ID using MFA (FIDO2 or Microsoft Authenticator) before any Azure resource access
Privileged roles (Owner, Contributor, Key Vault Administrator, etc.) are not permanently assigned — eligible via PIM only
Engineer requests privileged access via PIM — specifies duration (max 4 hours), justification, and approver
PIM notifies designated approver; approval granted, activation time-bounded and fully audited
All Azure service-to-service authentication uses system-assigned Managed Identities — no stored credentials or service account passwords
Quarterly access reviews run via Entra ID Governance — resource owners certify or revoke standing access
Defender for Identity monitors all identity activity; anomalous behaviour (unusual privilege use, lateral movement) triggers Sentinel alert and SOAR playbook
problemStatement: Without a standardised IAM/PAM framework, teams provision standing privileged access, share service account credentials, and operate outside the corporate identity plane — creating exploitable privilege escalation paths, audit failures, and inability to respond rapidly to identity-based incidents.
securityConsiderations: No standing privileged roles are permitted in production — all elevated access must be JIT via PIM
Service accounts with passwords are prohibited — all workload identities must use Managed Identities or certificate-based authentication
Breakglass accounts must be documented, MFA-exempt (FIDO2 hardware key only), and access triggers immediate SOC alert
All PIM activations, access reviews, and role assignments must be exported to Sentinel for long-term audit retention (7 years)
Third-party vendor access must be provisioned via Entra External Identities with time-bounded access packages — never via shared accounts
